claims.quote: The David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA is uses the AAMC's Visiting Student Learning Opportunities ( VSLO ) program, to receive visiting student applications. To apply to our institution, please complete a VSLO application for your preferred electives and dates.
claims.claimText: UCLA DGSOM uses AAMC VSLO to receive visiting student applications.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Visiting student elective application via VSLO is core Tier 1 USCE.

claims.quote: Students in their fourth year at a U.S. medical school. VSLO applicants must be in good academic standing and actively progressing toward a Doctor of Medicine degree at an affiliated LCME-accredited institution, or a Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine degree at an affiliated COCA-accredited AACOM Member Institution.
claims.claimText: Eligibility: MS4 from affiliated LCME or COCA-accredited US schools, good academic standing.
claims.limitations: Use of 'affiliated' may further restrict applicant pool — not enumerated on this page.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Eligibility statement for visiting MS4 applicants is Tier 1 USCE.

claims.quote: Due to limited course availability, our international VSLO elective program is now only open to pre-selected students from schools with which we have established reciprocal exchange agreements.
claims.claimText: International VSLO is restricted to pre-selected students from schools with reciprocal exchange agreements.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit refusal of unaffiliated international applicants is a Tier 1 negative-evidence claim.

claims.quote: UCLA has an expansive clinical footprint spanning the greater Los Angeles area, giving you a wide spectrum of patient care learning opportunities. As a visiting student , you’ll meet Angelenos, folks from around the globe, from all walks of life, and experience the diversity of the city while you are at it.
claims.claimText: UCLA offers clinical electives to visiting students across its Los Angeles clinical footprint.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Definite offer of visiting student clinical electives at the named medical school.

claims.quote: The David Geffen School of Medicine (DGSOM) at UCLA uses the AAMC Visiting Student Learning Opportunities ( VSLO ) program, to receive applications from US medical and osteopathic students.
claims.claimText: DGSOM at UCLA uses AAMC VSLO to receive applications from US medical and osteopathic students.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Page describes the visiting medical student elective program for 4th-year US medical/osteopathic students via VSLO — Tier 1 USCE.

claims.quote: Our electives are open to fourth-year students only.
claims.claimText: Electives open to fourth-year students only.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Eligibility statement defining year-of-training audience for the visiting-student electives — Tier 1.

claims.quote: VSLO applicants must be in good academic standing and actively progressing toward a Doctor of Medicine degree at an affiliated LCME-accredited institution, or a Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine degree at an affiliated COCA-accredited AACOM Member Institution.
claims.claimText: Applicants must be enrolled at an LCME-accredited MD or COCA-accredited DO institution in good standing.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: School-accreditation eligibility for visiting medical students — Tier 1.

claims.quote: Applicants must have completed their core clerkships.
claims.claimText: Applicants must have completed core clerkships before rotating.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Prerequisite eligibility for the elective — Tier 1.

claims.quote: To apply to our institution, please complete a VSLO application for your preferred electives and dates.
claims.claimText: Apply via VSLO application for preferred electives/dates.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application route for visiting students — Tier 1.

claims.quote: Typical three-week blocks for electives.
claims.claimText: Typical three-week elective blocks.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Elective duration — Tier 1.

claims.quote: Can I complete multiple rotations? Yes, students are permitted to complete up to 3 electives or 9 weeks of coursework.
claims.claimText: Students may complete up to 3 electives or 9 weeks total.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Maximum visit duration — Tier 1.

claims.quote: On the same day, you accept your offer in VSLO, you must submit a non-refundable payment of $300/elective electronically.
claims.claimText: Non-refundable $300 per elective payment due upon offer acceptance.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit elective fee — Tier 1.

claims.quote: USMLE Step 1 Score Report or COMPLEX-USA Report - Please upload a copy of your USMLE Step 1/COMLEX-USA score report.
claims.claimText: USMLE Step 1 or COMLEX-USA score report required.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Document requirement — Tier 1 USCE eligibility detail.

claims.quote: Required CertificationsCertifications must cover you throughout your proposed elective time frame.Valid OSHA CertificationValid ACLS or BLSValid Mask Fit Test
claims.claimText: Required certifications: OSHA, ACLS or BLS, Mask Fit Test.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Required certifications — Tier 1.

claims.quote: Letter of Recommendation - A letter of recommendation is required for all electives. This letter should be from a clinical faculty member and should speak to your clinical abilities but does not have to be from the department in which you are applying unless otherwise stated.
claims.claimText: One letter of recommendation from a clinical faculty member is required for all electives.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: LOR requirement — Tier 1.

claims.quote: Proof of Personal Health Insurance - You are required to submit evidence of personal health insurance (hospitalization and major medical care) during the time of the proposed clerkship. Health insurance for visitors is mandatory prior to enrollment but is NOT offered through the school.
claims.claimText: Personal health insurance is required; not provided by the school.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Insurance requirement — Tier 1.

claims.quote: AAMC Standard Immunization Form - Please note that UCLA requires TB clearance within a year of your proposed elective time frame.
claims.claimText: AAMC Immunization Form and TB clearance within a year required.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Immunization/TB requirement — Tier 1.

claims.quote: you may contact our office via email at dgsomelectives@mednet.ucla.edu
claims.claimText: Visiting student office contact email: dgsomelectives@mednet.ucla.edu
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Program-specific contact email — Tier 1.

claims.quote: Applications must be submitted in VSLO at least two and a half months (75 days) prior to the start date of the elective.
claims.claimText: Applications must be submitted at least 75 days before elective start.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application timing rule — Tier 1.

claims.quote:
VSLO Catalog Opens for Viewing: January 26th at 8am PST
Applications Open: March 9th 2026 at 8am PST
claims.claimText: 2026-2027 catalog opens Jan 26; applications open March 9, 2026 at 8am PST.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application cycle dates — Tier 1.

claims.quote: to receive applications from US medical and osteopathic students
claims.claimText: This VSLO program receives applications from US medical and osteopathic students (page is US-only stream).
claims.limitations: This page is the U.S.-students VSLO route; international students may be addressed elsewhere on the site.

claims.quote: I already graduated from medical school. Am I eligible to rotate? No. Visiting student rotations are designed for students who are enrolled in their final year of medical school and have already completed one full year of in-hospital clinical training.
claims.claimText: Already-graduated physicians (e.g., IMG graduates) are not eligible to rotate via the visiting student program.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Explicit refusal of graduates — Tier 1 negative evidence for IMG-graduate observership lane.

claims.quote: You can have 6 active elective, site, and/or date selection choices. Additional applications submitted beyond the limit will be rejected.
claims.claimText: Applicants may have up to 6 active elective/site/date selections.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application volume cap — Tier 1.

claims.quote: Training sites are located all over the city. The elective name on VSLO has the location listed- E.g., UCLA, Harbor UCLA, Olive View UCLA, and Santa Monica are all seperate elective locations.
claims.claimText: Elective sites include UCLA, Harbor-UCLA, Olive View-UCLA, and Santa Monica.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Site list for visiting students — Tier 1.

claims.quote: Can I apply to electives if I haven't yet taken Step 1 or COMLEX 1? Yes. We will require proof of a passing Step 1 or COMLEX 1 score before your rotation begins.
claims.claimText: Passing USMLE Step 1 or COMLEX 1 score required before rotation begins (not before applying).
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Step 1 timing rule — Tier 1.

claims.quote: Although the David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA does not provide housing
claims.claimText: DGSOM at UCLA does not provide housing for visiting students.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Housing policy — Tier 1.

claims.quote:
Links to different medical school scholarship opportunities for individual departments within the DGSOM system are listed here:
UCLA Anesthesiology and Perioperative Medicine Visiting Student Scholarship Program
claims.claimText: DGSOM lists departmental visiting-student scholarship programs (Anesthesiology, EM, Head & Neck, IM, Neurology, Neurosurgery, OB/Gyn, Ophthalmology, Peds, Psychiatry, Radiology, Rad Onc, Surgery, Surgery HBCU, Urology, Harbor-UCLA OB/Gyn & Anesth).
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Scholarship resources for visiting students — Tier 1.

futureLaneSignals.quote: The first of its kind in the U.S. and newly launched, the UCLA Space Medicine Fellowship aims to develop the next generation of flight surgeons who will support the health, safety, and well-being of human space flight and planetary expeditions.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: UCLA Space Medicine Fellowship — newly launched program training flight surgeons.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship program info — post-residency trainee track, Tier 2.

futureLaneSignals.quote: The two-year program will include rotations at SpaceX and a specialized engineering curriculum with the California Institute of Technology (Caltech) and NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), which is managed by Caltech.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Two-year fellowship with rotations at SpaceX and curriculum with Caltech and NASA JPL.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship duration and partner sites — trainee Tier 2.

futureLaneSignals.quote: The two-year program begins in July 2022, with its inaugural fellow Dr. Haig Aintablian, who recently completed his emergency medicine residency at the David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Program began July 2022 with inaugural fellow from DGSOM EM residency.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship start-date / inaugural fellow — Tier 2 trainee track.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Applications for subsequent years will begin in November 2022 and be open to graduates of four-year ACGME/OGME accredited Emergency Medicine residencies who are eligible for a California medical license.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Eligibility: graduates of 4-yr ACGME/OGME accredited EM residencies eligible for CA medical license.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship eligibility requirements — Tier 2 trainee track.

futureLaneSignals.quote: “We are excited to be joining forces with Caltech, JPL and SpaceX to prepare future flight surgeons to deal with the unique medical challenges posed for people in space and the effects of space travel on the human body in this quickly growing field,” said Dr. Jo Feldman, director of the fellowship.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Dr. Jo Feldman is director of the fellowship.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship director — Tier 2 trainee program leadership.

futureLaneSignals.quote: UCLA's newly launched Space Medicine Fellowship — the first of its kind in the nation — will soon begin training members of this new generation of flight surgeons. The unique two-year program, which has accepted its first fellow, involves rotations at aerospace company SpaceX, completion of a specialized engineering curriculum developed by Caltech and NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory, and biomechanical engineering training at UCLA's Samueli School of Engineering.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: UCLA Space Medicine Fellowship — newly launched two-year program training flight surgeons; first of its kind in the nation.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Describes a post-residency fellowship program (Space Medicine Fellowship) — Tier 2 trainee content.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Dr. Haig Aintablian, who recently completed his residency in emergency medicine at the David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA, has been chosen as the program's inaugural fellow and will begin his training in July 2022.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Inaugural fellow (post-EM residency) begins training July 2022.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Names the inaugural fellow and start date — fellowship trainee Tier 2 content.

futureLaneSignals.quote: The David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA launched the LGBTQ fellowship in 2019 to address the community’s specific health care needs and inequities.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: DGSOM at UCLA launched an LGBTQ Fellowship in 2019 focused on health care needs of the LGBTQ community.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Article describes a post-graduate fellowship program at DGSOM UCLA — Tier 2 trainee/fellowship content.

futureLaneSignals.quote: our program is focused on primary care physicians who are generally on the frontline of care.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: LGBTQ Fellowship is focused on primary care physicians.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Audience is post-residency primary care physicians — Tier 2 fellowship.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Fellows get training in a variety of clinical and non-clinical areas such as primary care, transgender care, HIV treatment and management, hepatitis B and C treatment, high-resolution anoscopy, urology, gynecology and gender-affirming surgery, substance abuse prevention and treatment, mental and behavioral health, and more.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Fellowship covers primary care, transgender care, HIV, hepatitis, anoscopy, urology, gynecology, gender-affirming surgery, substance abuse, mental/behavioral health.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Curriculum description for fellowship trainees — Tier 2.

futureLaneSignals.quote: The program consists of three core themes:Medical knowledge, disease prevention and clinical careAdvocacy, leadership and counselingSocial, cultural and behavioral health
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Three core themes: medical knowledge/clinical care; advocacy/leadership/counseling; social/cultural/behavioral health.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship program structure — Tier 2.

futureLaneSignals.quote: George Yen, MD, director of the program
futureLaneSignals.claimText: George Yen, MD is the director of the LGBTQ Fellowship program.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Identifies fellowship program director — Tier 2 trainee context.

futureLaneSignals.quote: One of the components of the fellowship addresses behavioral health through coursework and workshops offered by UCLA partner Antioch University, a master’s program with a designated LGBTQ+ affirming track for future therapists.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Behavioral health component delivered in partnership with Antioch University LGBTQ+ affirming master's track.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Describes fellowship coursework partnership — Tier 2.
